catalog.name: Nangijala — Cherry Valley
catalog.use: Main overworld background bed: daytime exploring of the home valley (village, meadows, cherry orchards). The default track the player hears most, so it must be beautiful, calm, and never fatiguing.
catalog.narrative: Nangijala is the land the Lionheart brothers wake up in — an afterlife valley of cherry blossoms and campfires. The music says: you have arrived somewhere kind, but it remembers the crossing; a thread of melancholy runs under the warmth.

layers.name: Combat — Drums of the Valley
layers.description: The same Cherry Valley theme at battle intensity: driving war drums and taiko join, a low brass ostinato runs under the melody, strings turn to urgent tremolo. Crossfade in when combat starts — the melody stays recognizably the same, so the world never 'changes song'.
style_delta: driving war drums and taiko hits keeping the same tempo
low brass ostinato under the same main melody
forceful battle energy, same key and structure

catalog.prompt: A warm, mythical orchestral fantasy theme for the overworld of a storybook adventure game, set in a peaceful afterlife valley of cherry blossoms, green meadows and campfire smoke. Cinematic but intimate, in the spirit of Howard Shore's Shire music and gentle Nordic folk. Structure: a solo wooden flute opens alone with a simple, singable melody over a soft string pad and sparse fingerpicked harp; the full theme then blooms in warm legato strings with the harp keeping flowing eighth-note arpeggios; a wide-eyed middle section lifts with a distant French horn countermelody and a soft wordless-choir-like pad, the emotional high point; then the theme settles back down to the quiet solo flute and strings for a gentle, unresolved, loop-friendly ending that can flow back into the beginning. A faint, soft frame-drum heartbeat keeps a clear gentle pulse throughout. Strictly instrumental. D major, 76 BPM, 4/4. High-fidelity, lush, spacious cinematic mix with soft dynamics and a touch of wistful melancholy under the warmth.

sections.name: awakening
sections.description: Solo wooden flute states the main melody over a soft string pad and sparse harp. The player's quietest moment — arrival, morning light.
sync_hints: Safe entry point for scene fade-in; ambience (birds, wind) should sit above the sparse mix here.
Align slow environment events (petal falls, drifting smoke) to downbeats_s.

sections.name: valley_theme
sections.description: The theme blooms: warm legato strings carry the melody, harp keeps flowing eighth-note arpeggios, frame-drum heartbeat becomes audible.
sync_hints: Good moment for the world to feel alive: NPC activity, animals, waterwheel motion synced to beats_s.
Footstep SFX pitched to sfx_safe_notes read as part of the arrangement here.

sections.name: wonder
sections.description: Emotional high point: French horn countermelody and soft choir-like pad widen the sound. Vista moment.
sync_hints: Best section for reveals (vista opens, title card, quest banner) — trigger on this section's first downbeat.
Use events.peaks inside this window for one-shot sparkle/light effects.

sections.name: homecoming
sections.description: The theme returns, gentler — strings soften, harp thins out. Warmth with the melancholy thread most audible.
sync_hints: Good window for quiet dialogue or journal UI — mix leaves headroom for voice/UI sounds.

sections.name: return_to_stillness
sections.description: Back to solo flute and pad, unresolved cadence; engineered to flow back into 'awakening' on loop.
sync_hints: Loop seam lives here: crossfade loop.recommended.crossfade_ms from end_s into 0.0.
Avoid triggering big scripted moments in this window — the bed is at its thinnest.
